mission: Close the remaining structural gaps in the late-time eta_IO theorem chain

gap_1_gravitational_constant.statement: G is not a genuine remaining gap in the surviving eta route. Once the theorem is written in terms of the derived physical-density slot omega_b,geom, the active projected H0 and Omega_m path is no longer used. The late-time eta theorem depends only on omega_b,geom, T_obs, and the standard particle/thermodynamic constants in the conversion prefactor.
gap_1_gravitational_constant.safe_statement: Do not list G as an additional active framework debt beyond the standard omega_b-to-number-density conversion prefactor.

gap_2_baryon_counting_law.status: derived / scoped under Premise 2 standard low-temperature baryonic matter
gap_2_baryon_counting_law.statement: On the late-time baryonic dust sector, baryon number density is the rest-mass density divided by the mean mass per baryon, up to thermal and binding/composition corrections. Thermal corrections at T_obs are negligible; composition corrections are small and quantifiable.
gap_2_baryon_counting_law.formula: n_b = rho_b / m_bar * [1 + O(k_B T/(m_p c^2)) + O(E_bind/(m_p c^2))]

mass_conventions.statement: The remaining normalization ambiguity is a standard baryon-mass convention at the 10^-3 level, not a live structural theorem gap.

closed_late_time_chain.status: derived / scoped plus standard particle constants
closed_late_time_chain.preferred_convention: mean mass per baryon on the derived helium-composition branch
closed_late_time_chain.statement: Under the surviving omega_b,geom + T_obs route and the standard low-temperature baryon-counting law, the late-time eta theorem is closed. The only remaining non-IO inputs are universal/particle constants and the mean baryon-mass convention.

what_remains_open.status: not part of the late-time eta closure
items: a purely internal derivation of the baryon mass scale
an independent chiral-boundary derivation of eta rather than the inventory route
